ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: Nyini ti, má am tana koko se ka, ‘Ye ya dí nzu wɔ?’ wara ‘Ya nú nzu wɔ?’ wara ‘Ya wúra nzu wɔ?’
translated: It is not that we say such words: "Do you eat food here?" or "Do you give food here?" or "Do you sell food here?"

ref: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
text: Na bara be aba Yesu dɔ, i ti má Yuda­fɔ, i ti Kanana­fɔ ɔ, bo i fite nyini awɔrɛ n nu. Na wɔ bo afere Yesu, ase i ka, “M Mibiɛ, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma, si m aŋunfɔ. Ajinim wo m wa baa­bara ŋu, sukere i yaari agaya.”
translated: Then in the morning, when Jesus came, he had sent Judas and Kanana, and he sought the place where he had stayed the night. Then the people came to meet Jesus, so he said, "I am he, the Son of the promised David; give me something to eat. Do you see me as one who takes bread? Truly I am the light of the world."

ref: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Am fa sin fite, baa­bara n awu má, wɔ daafi wɔ.” Ama, na ba siri i.
translated: Now they say, "We plow our fields, youths do not dance, they take care of themselves." Ama, then she listened.

ref: The field is the world, and the good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom. The weeds are those who belong to the evil one,
text: Fie n la durunya ni, na nvoni n la minɛm bo bu fa Nyɛmɛ yo bu fɛmɛ ni. Na jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n la Sitana dɛɛ minɛm ni.
translated: We are in the village now, and the people we are talking about are those who make Nyɛmɛ be worshiped. And the chiefs we are talking about are Sitana's people.

ref: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
text: “Ahin sɔnɔ n la atin bɔnɔ ye i jɔjɔ wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu sɔ a? Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro fa minɛm satiɛ ni bu ŋgasi ce, sin Nyɛmɛ ŋu.”
translated: What word did you say that came and you asked before Chief Nyɛmɛ from this statement? But you did not start by taking our honor in the chief's house, only Nyɛmɛ is here.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: na ba wo dɔ haari Fɛmɛ Herode aba awu. Ahin yoori sɔ ni, i ma kabo Micɛra n ma i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n yo jɔjɔɔri n yo yii­wa a. I ŋa, “Ma fere m wa n afite Ijipiti asiɛn n su.” Kabo Fɛmɛ Herode ma bu kuunri nbaa­biɛsɔ kan kanm ni
translated: Now they told Queen Herodias’ people to bring these. When she came there, she did not allow Mary to sing so that her voice could be heard, and she sang with joy there. She said, “Do not let me go to the church every time to worship.” But Queen Herodias was angry because of that matter there.

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: Ambɛrɛ nu, ŋma ye i wa sere i diire, ye i mi kéte na i fá yabuɛ ma i wa n a? Ai, be yo má sɔ.
translated: Ambɛrɛ now, but say they are speaking and they hear, say they ask me and they cause trouble but they are here? Oh, you say you don't know.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: Na awuru­fɔ­biɛ ni akɔɔm aba i dɔ, aba ase i ka, ‘Ya mibiɛ, m ŋa ya sandiiri nvoni kpa fie n nu e. Ye jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n fiteeri ni, kɔ saŋgaari nu a?’
translated: Now at the gathering of the elders' talk, the people who came and did the work said, "O my people, I will take a sword from the chief's house and give it to you. Are you afraid of the enemy, or what do you say?"

ref: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
text: Na Petoro akaaki ase i ka, “Mini sé má sɔ da. Nzɛn haari wɔ yo daka ka mini ni wɔrɔ kɛrɛ saŋga wú mmɔɔ, ŋka ya wú wɔ.” Na i susu­fɔm n kɛrɛ ase sɔ. Kabo Yesu jeke tiiri na i kɔ sereeri Nyɛmɛ ni
translated: And Peter addressed the assembly, saying, “I cannot hear properly.” Because you listen so that when I have a severe ear problem, you might hear me? And he prayed that the deaf people might hear. Then Jesus was present and he worked a miracle of power.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Bu kɔ juuri awuru n nu ni, na ba se Yesu ka Simon sibara la má i ŋu, yakɔkɔ abo i, i da sɔrɔ.
translated: But now the people say that they do not sleep at night, and Jesus told Simon to cast his net into the sea, and at dawn the sun rose.

ref: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
text: Na minɛm ni kun ase i ka, “Ti fɛn, u ni, ni u niɛmam jina dasin koro ka bu jɔjɔ u dɔ.”
translated: Now those who do good deeds say, "When death comes, you are, and your deeds will be declared from the mouth that it will not consume your wealth."

ref: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.
text: Na Yesu ase i ka, “Bra.” Na Petoro afite lee n nu sunati nzue n su sukɔ Yesu dɔ.
translated: And Jesus said, "Brother." And Peter's disciples came to listen to the word, and they said about Jesus that he is.

ref: So they kept it to themselves. But they wondered what he meant by the words “raised from death.”
text: Na ba de i nɔaniɛ ni, ama, na ba bakati abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, “Ye nzu la ka, i bá wu na i téŋge i yiwee n nu a?”
translated: Now they have come to listen here, but now they said those words to us, “Why are you talking to us? You neither do nor understand, and you tell us again?”

ref: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
text: Nyini cɛɛn n naasɔ, dika bibiri saŋga, na minɛm afa tukpaki­fɔm kɛrɛ ni minɛm bo ajinim wo bu ŋu n aba Yesu dɔ.
translated: This is what we say: like great fear, our unheard words exist in our hearts, causing us to worship Jesus Christ

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: Na wɔ niɛ abara ayɛ minɛm bo bu tana bara yɛɛri i ni, na wɔ se bu ka, “Am niɛ. M ni, ni m niɛmam n yaa.
translated: Now they speak of the matter that our words are not clearly known to those who listen to us, and they answer, "I say. I am, for I think I speak."

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: na ba wo dɔ haari Fɛmɛ Herode aba awu. Ahin yoori sɔ ni, i ma kabo Micɛra n ma i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n yo jɔjɔɔri n yo yii­wa a. I ŋa, “Ma fere m wa n afite Ijipiti asiɛn n su.” Kabo Fɛmɛ Herode ma bu kuunri nbaa­biɛsɔ kan kanm ni
translated: And they saw that Queen Herodias was doing evil. When the time came, she called Mary and brought her young daughter to dance in front of them. She said, “Do not refuse to give me the head of John the Baptist.” Then Queen Herodias swore a solemn oath.

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?

translated: Ambɛrɛ, why do you say it is like this? Do you strike me and take my pain yet I am here? Oh, you should not speak.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”

translated: Now those who speak about these people here who are, these deeds that they do: "O my people, I will go along the paths that build houses and live there. What is the good journey, or take another path there?"

translated: And Petoro stood up and said to them, "I tell you, do not fear. Remember the things I told you: that in the world you will struggle with demons, but you have power over them." And he commended his work to the Lord. Then Jesus came and said that he had served God well.
